title: HA tag enables highly efficient detection of heterologous proteins in Phaeodactylum tricornutum exconjugants
author: Jernej Turnšek
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.j7ncrme

steps.title: Spin down ~10 7 -10 8...
steps.instruction: Spin down ~10 7 -10 8 Pt cells for 10 min at 4000 rpm & 10 °C. Keep tubes on ice throughout the protocol.

steps.title: Resuspend pellets in 50-200 μL cell lysis buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellets in 50-200 μL cell lysis buffer: 50 mM Tris-HCl, 200 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 1 mM PMSF.

steps.title: Sonicate as follows
steps.instruction: Sonicate as follows: 30 sec on / 1 min off for 15 min. We use Bioruptor UCD-200TM.

steps.title: Use your favorite protein quantification assay
steps.instruction: Use your favorite protein quantification assay. We use ThermoFischer's Bradford assay .

steps.title: Separate supernatants and pellets/insoluble fractions by...
steps.instruction: Separate supernatants and pellets/insoluble fractions by centrifuging lysates 45 min at 13500 rpm & 10 °C. Resuspend pellets/insoluble fractions in 100 μL lysis buffer.

steps.title: Prepare MOPS running buffer as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare MOPS running buffer as follows: 665 mL MQ + 35 mL 20x MOPS buffer.

steps.title: We have had good experience with NuPage 4-12% Bis-Tris 1
steps.instruction: We have had good experience with NuPage 4-12% Bis-Tris 1.5 mm gels with 10 wells .

steps.title: Add 500 μL NuPAGE Antioxidant to the inner reservoir
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL NuPAGE Antioxidant to the inner reservoir.

steps.title: Separate proteins for 50 min at 200 V
steps.instruction: Separate proteins for 50 min at 200 V.

steps.title: Prepare transfer buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare transfer buffer: 40 mL NuPage Transfer Buffer (20x) , 160 mL methanol, 600 mL MQ, 800 μL NuPAGE Antioxidant . We do a wet transfer using XCell II TM Blot Module and PVDF membranes.

steps.title: Prepare supernatants as follows (40 μL total)
steps.instruction: Prepare supernatants as follows (40 μL total): 10 μL NuPage LDS Sample Buffer (4x) , 4 μL NuPAGE Sample Reducing Agent (10x) , 40 μg protein, MQ. Prepare pellets/insoluble fractions as follows (40 uL total): 10 μL NuPage LDS Sample Buffer (4x) , 4 μL NuPAGE Sample Reducing Agent (10x) , 4 μL pellet/insoluble fraction suspension, 22 μL MQ. Incubate at 70 °C for 15 min.

steps.title: Soak ~7 pads in transfer buffer
steps.instruction: Soak ~7 pads in transfer buffer.

steps.title: Activate PVDF membranes in methanol (~1 min is good)
steps.instruction: Activate PVDF membranes in methanol (~1 min is good). Wash in MQ, leave in transfer buffer. Leave filter papers that come with each membrane soaking in transfer buffer while you're activating membranes.

steps.title: Transfer proteins for 1 h at 30 V
steps.instruction: Transfer proteins for 1 h at 30 V.

steps.title: Dissassemble your gels and stack a transfer sandwich
steps.instruction: Dissassemble your gels and stack a transfer sandwich. If transferring from 2 gels simultaneously, we recommend assembling your sandwich as follows: a

steps.title: Cut top right corner of each...
steps.instruction: Cut top right corner of each membrane to track membrane faces containing proteins.

steps.title: Wash each membrane twice with 20...
steps.instruction: Wash each membrane twice with 20 mL MQ for 5 min. All washing and incubation steps at RT were performed on a rocking platform set to 1 (very gentle rocking).

steps.title: Prapare blocking buffer. For 2 membranes
steps.instruction: Prapare blocking buffer. For 2 membranes: 10 mL MQ, 4 mL "Part A", 6 mL "Part B".

steps.title: Block at least 1 h at room temperature
steps.instruction: Block at least 1 h at room temperature. Use 10 mL/membrane. The result in this protocol was obtained after a 2 day blocking step at 4 °C followed by another ~1 h 15 min RT incubation period.

steps.title: Wash each membrane twice with 20...
steps.instruction: Wash each membrane twice with 20 mL MQ for 5 min.

steps.title: Prepare 1:10000 dilution of HA tag...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1:10000 dilution of HA tag monoclonal antibody : 14 mL MQ, 4 mL "Part A", 2 mL "Part B", 2 µL antibody stock.

steps.title: Use 10 mL antibody solution per...
steps.instruction: Use 10 mL antibody solution per membrane and incubate ~3 hr.

steps.title: Prepare 160 mL wash solution
steps.instruction: Prepare 160 mL wash solution: 150 mL MQ + 10 mL Wash Solution (16x). Wash each membrane 4 times in 20 mL for 5 min.

steps.title: Incubate each membrane in 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Incubate each membrane in 10 mL anti-mouse secondary antibody solution for 1 hr.

steps.title: Prepare 160 mL wash solution
steps.instruction: Prepare 160 mL wash solution: 150 mL MQ + 10 mL Wash Solution (16x). Wash each membrane 4 times in 20 mL for 5 min.

steps.title: Wash each membrane 3 times in...
steps.instruction: Wash each membrane 3 times in 20 mL MQ for 2 min.

steps.title: Remove excess liquid, place a membrane...
steps.instruction: Remove excess liquid, place a membrane on transparent plastic foil and cover it evenly with 2 mL Novex CDP-Star alkaline phosphatase substrate.

steps.title: Cover with alufoil and incubate ~5 min
steps.instruction: Cover with alufoil and incubate ~5 min.

steps.title: Remove excess liquid and cover with another transparent plastic sheet
steps.instruction: Remove excess liquid and cover with another transparent plastic sheet.

steps.title: We use LI-COR's C-DiGit Blot Scanner
steps.instruction: We use LI-COR's C-DiGit Blot Scanner .

steps.title: 5 genotype+ Pt exconjugants were screened for protein-HA expression
steps.instruction: 5 genotype+ Pt exconjugants were screened for protein-HA expression. The left and right image below were obtained with superntants and insoluble fractions, respectively. a
